 {"id":"bd-n777","title":"Timer beads for scheduled agent callbacks","description":"## Problem

Agents frequently need to wait for external events (CI completion, PR reviews, artifact builds) but have no good mechanism:
- `sleep N` blocks and is unreliable (often times out at 8+ minutes)
- Polling wastes context and is easy to forget
- No way to survive session restarts

## Proposal: Timer Beads

A new bead type or field that represents a scheduled callback:

### Creating timers
```bash
bd timer create --in 30s --callback "Check CI run 12345" --issue bd-xyz
bd timer create --at "2025-12-20T08:00:00" --callback "Morning standup"
bd timer create --in 5m --on-expire "tmux send-keys -t dave 'bd show bd-xyz'"
```

### Timer storage
- Store in beads (survives restarts)
- Fields: `expires_at`, `callback_description`, `on_expire_command`, `linked_issue`
- Status: pending, fired, cancelled

### Deacon integration
The Deacon daemon monitors timer beads:
1. Wakes on next timer expiry
2. Executes `on_expire` command (e.g., tmux send-keys to interrupt agent)
3. Marks timer as fired
4. Optionally updates linked issue

### Use cases
- CI monitoring: "ping me when build completes"
- PR reviews: "check back in 1 hour"
- Scheduled tasks: "remind me at EOD to sync"
- Blocking waits: agent registers callback instead of sleeping

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] Timer bead type or field design
- [ ] `bd timer create/list/cancel` commands
- [ ] Deacon timer monitoring loop
- [ ] tmux integration for agent interrupts
- [ ] Survives daemon restarts","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-19T23:05:33.051861-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:19:48.087482-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:19:48.087482-08:00","close_reason":"Will not implement - Gas Town uses a different approach for timed events"}
 {"id":"bd-ncwo","title":"Ghost resurrection: remote status:closed wins during git merge","description":"During bd sync, the 3-way git merge sometimes keeps remote's status:closed instead of local's status:tombstone. This causes ghost issues to resurrect even when tombstones exist.

Root cause: Git 3-way merge doesn't understand tombstone semantics. If base had closed, local changed to tombstone, and remote has closed, git might keep remote's version.

The early tombstone check in importer.go only prevents CREATION when tombstones exist in DB. But if applyDeletionsFromMerge hard-deletes the tombstones before import runs (because they're not in the merged result), the check doesn't help.

Potential fixes:
1. Make tombstones 'win' in the beads merge driver (internal/merge/merge.go)
2. Don't hard-delete tombstones in applyDeletionsFromMerge if they're in the DB
3. Export tombstones to a separate file that's not subject to merge

Ghost issues: bd-cb64c226.*, bd-cbed9619.*","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-16T22:01:03.56423-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-17T16:11:17.070763-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}
 {"id":"bd-ndye","title":"mergeDependencies uses union instead of 3-way merge","description":"## Critical Bug

The `mergeDependencies` function in internal/merge/merge.go performs a UNION of left and right dependencies instead of a proper 3-way merge. This causes removed dependencies to be resurrected.

### Root Cause

```go
// Current code (lines 795-816):
func mergeDependencies(left, right []Dependency) []Dependency {
    // Just unions left + right
    // NEVER REMOVES anything
    // Doesn't even look at base!
}
```

And `mergeIssue` (line 579) doesn't pass `base`:
```go
result.Dependencies = mergeDependencies(left.Dependencies, right.Dependencies)
```

### Impact

If:
- Base has dependency D
- Left removes D (intentional)
- Right still has D (stale)

Current: D is in result (resurrection!)
Correct: Left removed it, D should NOT be in result

This breaks Gas Town's workflow and data integrity. Closed means closed.

### Fix

Change `mergeDependencies` to take `base` and do proper 3-way merge:
- If dep was in base and removed by left → exclude (left wins)
- If dep was in base and removed by right → exclude (right wins)
- If dep wasn't in base and added by either → include
- If dep was in base and both still have it → include

Key principle: **REMOVALS ARE AUTHORITATIVE**

### Files to Change

1. internal/merge/merge.go:
   - `mergeDependencies(left, right)` → `mergeDependencies(base, left, right)`
   - `mergeIssue` line 579: pass `base.Dependencies`

### Related

This also explains why `ProtectLocalExportIDs` in importer is defined but never used - the protection was never actually implemented.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-18T23:15:54.475872-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T23:21:10.709571-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-18T23:21:10.709571-08:00"}
-{"id":"bd-nim5","title":"Detect/prevent child→parent dependency anti-pattern","description":"## Problem

When filing issues with dependencies, it's easy to fall into the "temporal trap":
- Thinking "Phase 1 comes before Phase 2"
- Writing `bd dep add phase1 phase2`
- But that means "phase1 depends on phase2" (backwards!)

A specific variant: children depending on their parent epic. This is ALWAYS wrong because:
1. Parent-child relationship already captures the dependency (parent closes when children done)
2. Child→parent dep creates a block: child can't start because parent is "open"
3. Parent can't close because children aren't done
4. Deadlock

## Solution

### 1. Prevent at creation (`bd dep add`)

When user runs `bd dep add X Y`:
- Check if X is a child of Y (X.id starts with Y.id + ".")
- If so, error: "Cannot add dependency: X is already a child of Y. Children inherit dependency on parent completion via hierarchy."

### 2. Detect in `bd doctor`

Add a check that finds all cases where:
- Issue A depends on Issue B
- A.id matches pattern B.id + ".*" (A is child of B)

Report as: "Child→parent dependency detected (anti-pattern)"
Offer repair: "Remove N backwards dependencies? [y/N]"

## Implementation

- `dep_add.go`: Add parent-child check before adding dependency
- `doctor.go`: Add backwards-dep detection to health checks

 {"id":"bd-nl2","title":"No logging/debugging for tombstone resurrection events","description":"Per the design document bd-zvg Open Question 1: Should resurrection log a warning? Recommendation was Yes. Currently, when an expired tombstone loses to a live issue (resurrection), there is no logging or debugging output. This makes it hard to understand why an issue reappeared. Recommendation: Add optional debug logging when resurrection occurs, e.g., Issue bd-abc resurrected (tombstone expired).  {"id":"bd-nqyp","title":"mol-beads-release","description":"Release checklist for beads version {{version}}.

This molecule ensures all release steps are completed properly.
Variable: {{version}} - target version (e.g., 0.35.0)

## Step: update-release-notes
Update cmd/bd/info.go with release notes for {{version}}.

Add a new VersionChange entry at the top of versionChanges slice:
```go
{
    Version: "{{version}}",
    Date:    "YYYY-MM-DD",
    Changes: []string{
        "NEW: Feature description",
        "FIX: Bug fix description",
        "IMPROVED: Enhancement description",
    },
},
```

Run `git log --oneline v<previous>..HEAD` to see what changed.

## Step: update-changelog
Update CHANGELOG.md with detailed release notes.

Add a new section after [Unreleased]:
```markdown
## [{{version}}] - YYYY-MM-DD

### Added
- **Feature name** (issue-id) - Description

### Changed
- **Change description** (issue-id)

### Fixed
- **Bug fix** (issue-id) - Description
```

Sort by importance, not chronologically.
Needs: update-release-notes

## Step: bump-version
Run the version bump script.

```bash
./scripts/bump-version.sh {{version}}
```

This updates version in all files:
- cmd/bd/version.go
- .claude-plugin/*.json
- integrations/beads-mcp/pyproject.toml
- npm-package/package.json
- Hook templates

Needs: update-changelog

## Step: run-tests
Run tests and verify lint passes.

```bash
go test -short ./...
```

CI will run full lint, but fix any obvious issues first.
Needs: bump-version

## Step: commit-release
Commit the release changes.

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "chore: bump version to v{{version}}"
```

Needs: run-tests

## Step: push-and-tag
Push commit and create release tag.

```bash
git push origin main
git tag v{{version}}
git push origin v{{version}}
```

This triggers GitHub Actions release workflow.
Needs: commit-release

## Step: wait-for-ci
Wait for GitHub Actions to complete.

Monitor: https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/actions

CI will:
- Build binaries via GoReleaser
- Create GitHub Release with assets
- Publish to npm (@beads/bd)
- Publish to PyPI (beads-mcp)
- Update Homebrew tap

Wait until all jobs succeed (~5-10 min).
Needs: push-and-tag

## Step: verify-release
Verify the release is complete.

```bash
# Check GitHub release
gh release view v{{version}}

# Check Homebrew
brew update && brew info steveyegge/beads/bd

# Check npm
npm view @beads/bd version

# Check PyPI
pip index versions beads-mcp
```

Needs: wait-for-ci

## Step: update-local
Update local installations.

```bash
# Upgrade Homebrew
brew upgrade steveyegge/beads/bd

# Or install from source
./scripts/bump-version.sh {{version}} --install

# Install MCP locally
pip install -e integrations/beads-mcp

# Restart daemons
pkill -f "bd daemon" || true
```

Verify: `bd --version` shows {{version}}
Needs: verify-release

## Step: manual-publish
(Optional) Manual publish if CI failed.

```bash
# npm (requires npm login)
./scripts/bump-version.sh {{version}} --publish-npm

# PyPI (requires TWINE credentials)
./scripts/bump-version.sh {{version}} --publish-pypi

# Or both
./scripts/bump-version.sh {{version}} --publish-all
```

Only needed if CI publishing failed.
Needs: wait-for-ci","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"molecule","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:29:39.087936-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T11:29:39.087936-08:00"}
